signInToSync: Sign in to sync across devices

hub.title: Settings
hub.description: Tune how midnite looks, locks, and runs your agents.

appearance.languageDescription: The language used across the app. Syncs to your account when you're signed in.
appearance.colourThemeDescription: Light, dark, follow your system, or switch automatically by time of day.
appearance.interfaceFontDescription: The font used for body text across the app. System fonts only — no download, applies instantly. Code, terminals, and the wordmark keep their own type.
appearance.fontPreview: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog — 0123456789
appearance.sideNavigationDescription: Lock the nav open or closed, or let it stay collapsed and expand on hover.
appearance.backgroundPatternDescription: The decorative backdrop drawn behind every page — plus the dashboard header, the landing screen, and the screensaver. The starfield adapts to your theme and accent.
appearance.intensityDescription: How visible the animated gradient backdrop is.
appearance.dynamicMotionDescription: Subtly animate the pattern and let it react to your cursor — the starfield swirls and fires, dots scatter, rulers track, gradients drift like clouds.
appearance.motionReducedNotice: Your Motion setting is “Reduced”, so the static pattern is shown instead until motion is allowed again.
appearance.accentDescription: Retints buttons, links, and focus rings across the app. Pick the brand gradient, a gradient preset, or a solid — or build your own. Adapts to light and dark.
appearance.densityDescription: Comfortable uses the default spacing; compact fits more on screen.
appearance.motionDescription: Follow your system's reduced-motion setting, minimise animation, or always animate.
appearance.shimmerCascadeDescription: Which way the live status-pill shimmer sweeps across the home screen and screensaver.
appearance.autoShowGuidesDescription: Show a page's product guide once, the first time you visit it. Turn this off to keep guides available only on demand from the assistant menu.
appearance.capitalisationDescription: How the wordmark is cased everywhere it renders. Trial each form against the font below.
appearance.wordmarkFontDescription: The font the midnite wordmark is set in across the app.

accentBuilder.secondaryAccentDescription: An independent second colour for secondary UI accents. Off by default.
accentBuilder.customGradientDescription: Build your own gradient — pick linear or conic, one colour (mono-shade) or up to three, and an angle.

profile.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
profile.changePasswordHint: Choose a new password for your account.
profile.linkedAccountsHint: Third-party providers you can sign in with. Linked automatically the first time you use “Continue with Google / GitHub”.

agents.providerUnconfigured: {name} is set as your AI provider but has no API key, so AI features (task triage, plan drafting, the heartbeat) will be skipped. Add a key under its API tab below, or switch the active provider.
agents.name: Name
agents.primaryDescriptionHint: The orchestrator's system prompt — how it should plan, delegate and decide what to work on.
agents.primaryDescriptionPlaceholder: You are the orchestrator. You triage incoming work, break it into tasks…
agents.heartbeatHint: A prompt the orchestrator runs on a schedule — for sweeps, check-ins or any standing task.
agents.intervalHint: How often the heartbeat prompt runs — from every hour up to once a month.
agents.heartbeatPromptPlaceholder: Review open tasks and surface anything that's stalled or needs a decision…
agents.parallelAgentsHint: How many agent sessions may run at once. Extra tasks queue until a slot frees up.
agents.agentsIntro: Every coding agent midnite knows about. Expand one to install or update its CLI (used to run task sessions) or, under the API tab, add your own key so that provider can power midnite's own AI features.
agents.noSubagentsHint: Add focused workers the orchestrator can delegate to.
agents.removeSubagentDescription: {name} will be deleted and the orchestrator can no longer delegate to it.
agents.sessionCliHint: The coding agent launched in a session terminal to run tasks.
agents.aiProviderHint: Powers midnite's own AI (triage, plan drafting, heartbeat). Add each provider's key in its card below.
agents.rolePlaceholder: Condenses long threads into a brief
agents.subDescriptionHint: The full system prompt for this subagent.
agents.subDescriptionPlaceholder: You are a summariser. Given a transcript, produce a tight, faithful summary…
agents.markdownHint: Markdown mode — written as the agent's prompt document.

apiTokens.secretWarning: Copy this token now — it will not be shown again.
apiTokens.secretUsage: Pass as Authorization: Bearer {token} on API requests.
apiTokens.title: API Tokens
apiTokens.description: Machine-readable tokens for CI/CD pipelines and scripted integrations.
apiTokens.empty: No API tokens yet. Create one to authenticate scripts and CI/CD pipelines.

mode.title: Autonomy mode
mode.description: Controls how midnite handles tool-use approvals during agent sessions.

manual.label: Manual
manual.description: Ask before every tool call. No automatic decisions.

guarded.label: Guarded
guarded.description: Auto-allow read-only tools (Read, Grep, Glob, LS). Ask for everything else.

autonomous.label: Autonomous
autonomous.description: Rules decide. Escalate only when no rule matches.

rules.title: Rules
rules.description: Durable allow/deny rules evaluated in guarded and autonomous modes.
rules.empty: No rules yet. Add one above to start customising tool behaviour.

history.title: History
history.description: Every tool-use decision — by you or the policy engine.

credentials.title: Workflow credentials
credentials.description: Secrets used by workflow integration nodes (Slack, SMTP, HTTP auth). Secret material is encrypted at rest and never returned by the API — only the name and type are visible here.

form.title: New credential
form.name: Name
form.oauthNote: Clicking “Authorize” will redirect you to the provider’s consent page. The credential will be saved automatically on return.

delete.title: Delete credential?
delete.description: "{name}" will be permanently removed. Any workflow node that references it will fail until updated.

data.title: Data
data.description: Back up and restore your whole midnite store — for disaster recovery or moving instances.

download.title: Download a backup
download.description: A portable .zip archive of every work domain, restorable onto this or a fresh instance.
download.includeSecretsDesc: — API keys, webhook signing secrets & workflow credentials, re-encrypted under a passphrase.
download.passphraseLabel: Passphrase (you’ll need it to restore — it’s never stored)
download.passphraseRequired: A passphrase is required to include secrets.
download.lockNoteIncluded: Secrets are re-encrypted under your passphrase — the raw instance key never leaves this box.
download.lockNoteExcluded: Secrets (API keys, tokens) are excluded — enable “Include secrets” to carry them, or reconfigure integrations after a restore.
download.lastBackup: Last backup: schema v{version} · {count} records across {domains} domains.

autoBackup.title: Scheduled auto-backup
autoBackup.description: Automatic timestamped backups on an interval, pruned to a retention count.
autoBackup.disabled: Scheduled auto-backup is off. Enable it by setting backup.enabled in midnite.json.

restore.title: Restore from a backup
restore.description: Upload an archive to preview it, then restore — merge keeps existing records, replace wipes them first.
restore.blocked: This archive was made by a newer midnite (schema v{schemaVersion}, {compat}) than this instance understands. Upgrade midnite, then retry — it can’t be imported safely.
restore.previewLabel: Preview — schema v{version} ({compat}): {count, plural, one {# record} other {# records}}
restore.passphraseLabel: Passphrase (to restore this archive’s secrets — leave blank to skip them)
restore.mergeDesc: — insert new records, keep existing ones.
restore.replaceDesc: — wipe the listed domains, then restore. Destructive.
restore.confirmLabel: Type replace to confirm wiping existing data:
restore.toastRestored: Restored {count, plural, one {# record} other {# records}} ({mode}).

autosave.title: Autosave
autosave.description: How often editors save your work automatically. The Save button always stays available and is enabled only when there are unsaved changes. Choose Off to save manually.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.description: Outbound webhooks — push task events to Slack, Discord, or any receiver.
integrations.empty: No webhook endpoints yet. Add one to push task events to a channel or service.

deliveriesPanel.empty: No deliveries yet. Move a matching task — or hit “Send test”.

secret.title: Signing secret
secret.description: Copy this now — it will not be shown again. Use it to verify the X-Midnite-Signature header.

inbound.title: Inbound sources
inbound.subtitle: Let GitHub, Linear, or any signed sender open tasks on your board.
inbound.empty: No inbound sources yet. Add one to receive external events.

created.title: Source created
created.description: Paste the signing secret into the sender's webhook config. It is shown once — rotate if you lose it.

repos.title: Repos
repos.description: Named checkouts the orchestrator runs agents against. A task's repo resolves to the checkout's folder when its session opens. Paths may use <home>~</home> for your home directory. Set <ownerRepo>owner/repo</ownerRepo> to enable GitHub webhook wiring.
repos.nameAndPathRequired: Both a name and a path are required.
repos.empty: No repos yet — add one below.

remove.title: Remove "{name}"?
remove.description: Tasks referencing it fall back to the default working directory.

webhook.title: GitHub webhook
webhook.description: Link a webhook-trigger workflow to receive GitHub pull_request events and automatically review PRs with Claude.
webhook.setOwnerRepo: Set owner/repo on this repo to enable payload filtering.
webhook.noWorkflows: No webhook-trigger workflows found. Install the AI Code Review template from Workflows → Templates.
webhook.saveSecret: Save the secret now — it will not be shown again.

steps.step1: Go to <target></target> → Settings → Webhooks → Add webhook.
steps.step2: Paste the URL above into the Payload URL field.
steps.step4: Paste the secret above into the Secret field.
steps.step5: Under “Which events…”, choose <selectEvents>Let me select individual events</selectEvents> and tick <pullRequests>Pull requests</pullRequests> only.

safety.title: Safety
safety.description: Guardrails for autonomous agents — pause, policy, caps, and blast-radius limits.

killSwitch.title: Kill switch & pause
killSwitch.description: Pause scheduling (running agents finish) or emergency-stop (abort in-flight agents, requeued).

spend.title: Spend, rate & blast-radius
spend.description: What blocks or denies agent work — configured, shown read-only.

recentDecisions.title: Recent decisions
recentDecisions.description: Latest act-path allow / deny / escalate decisions.

screenLock.inactivityTimeoutDescription: How long without any input before the screensaver appears. Any activity wakes it.
screenLock.cycleDurationDescription: How long each phrase is shown before the next is typed out — on the screensaver and the home screen.
screenLock.requirePasscodeDescription: Ask for a {length}-digit passcode to wake the screensaver. Stored only on this device — clear it any time if you forget it.
screenLock.onlyWhenLockedDescription: Skip the passcode for the idle screensaver — only ask when you lock manually with the power button.

manual.label: Manual
manual.description: Claude asks for approval before every tool call.

guarded.label: Guarded
guarded.description: Approval rules control which tools are auto-allowed; everything else waits.

autonomous.label: Autonomous
autonomous.description: All tool calls proceed without approval. Use with care.

security.autonomyModeDescription: Controls how much Claude acts without waiting for your explicit approval.
security.approvalRulesDescription: Rules are evaluated in order; first match wins. Active in Guarded mode.

personalization.aboutYouDescription: Tell midnite who you are — your role, how you like to work, the kinds of things you build. Agents can use this for context.
personalization.aboutPlaceholder: e.g. I'm a backend engineer who cares about small, well-tested commits…
personalization.workingDirectoryDescription: The folder session terminals fall back to when a task has no project directory of its own. Leave it unset to use the gateway's own directory.
personalization.notSet: Not set — uses the gateway's directory
personalization.agentGuidelinesDescription: Guidance applied to every session, layered on top of each project's own guidelines. Good for standing preferences — tone, conventions, things to always or never do.
personalization.guidelinesPlaceholder: e.g. Prefer functional style. Always run the linter before finishing. Keep PRs focused…

env.title: Environment
env.description: The local toolchain midnite needs, detected on the gateway host.
env.referenceDetected: Detected system is {host} — these {tab} tools are shown for reference.
env.referenceNoDetect: Shown for reference — couldn't detect the host OS.
env.runHint: The {action} command is typed below — press <kbd>Enter</kbd> to run it. Close anytime.

features.title: Features
features.description: Turn sections of the app on or off. Disabled features are hidden from the sidebar; if you open one directly you'll be prompted to re-enable it here. These groups mirror the sidebar's sections.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.taskUpdatesDescription: Get a desktop notification when a task needs your input or finishes. Works in the browser and the desktop app.
notifications.blockedHint: Notifications are blocked in your browser — allow them in site settings to enable this.

realtime.title: Realtime
realtime.bufferDescription: How many recent live events the gateway retains per channel so a briefly disconnected client can catch up instead of missing updates. Larger = longer catch-up window, more memory. Applied live (admin only); resets to the configured default on restart.

setup.title: Setup readiness
setup.description: Whether this install can run agents — live provider, secret-key, agent-CLI, pool and repo state. The same checklist drives the first-run prompt.
setup.loadError: Couldn't load setup status — is the gateway running?

updates.title: Updates
updates.channelDescription: Stable tracks tagged releases. Beta opts into pre-release builds to test the newest features early — expect rough edges. The update banner and the desktop auto-updater both follow this choice.

team.title: Teams
team.subtitle: Collaborate with others by joining or creating a team.
team.empty: You're not in any teams yet.

danger.heading: Danger zone
danger.confirm: This will permanently delete {name} and remove all members. Are you sure?
